action.reasoning: Observation: diagnose.py proves torch.load on train.pt works, but train.py in workspace is visibly truncated and every execution segfaults immediately; the root cause is file corruption, not data access. Analysis: we need to fully overwrite train.py with a compact complete script and validate it before training. To avoid another hidden truncation issue, also compile all scripts and run a tiny end-to-end train plus local predict smoke test in one command. Decision: rewrite train.py completely with a minimal PT-backed trainer, keep model/predict unchanged, then run py_compile, 1-epoch tiny training, create a local test.pt from val.pt, and execute predict.py. If successful, next iteration will submit eval immediately.
action.command:
python -m py_compile model.py train.py predict.py diagnose.py && python train.py --epochs 1 --train_limit 128 --val_limit 64 --batch_size 8 --emb_dim 64 --num_layers 2 --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --mmap && mkdir -p local_test && python - <<'PY'
import torch, os
obj = torch.load('/data/val.pt', map_location='cpu', weights_only=False)
out = {
    'node_features': obj['node_features'][:16],
    'edge_index': obj['edge_index'][:16],
    'edge_attr': obj['edge_attr'][:16],
}
torch.save(out, 'local_test/test.pt')
PY
python predict.py --data_path local_test --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output local_test/preds.json --batch_size 8 --mmap

command_output.stderr: bash: line 10:  2067 Segmentation fault      (core dumped) python train.py --epochs 1 --train_limit 128 --val_limit 64 --batch_size 8 --emb_dim 64 --num_layers 2 --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --mmap
